seoTitle: Centennial Park vs Cervantes — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Centennial Park and Cervantes. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Centennial Park vs Cervantes: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Centennial Park and Cervantes.
heading: Centennial Park vs Cervantes — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Centennial Park, Western Australia and Cervantes, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cervantes has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Centennial Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Centennial Park or Cervantes safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Centennial Park has a flood risk score of 45/100, while Cervantes scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Centennial Park scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Cervantes scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Centennial Park or Cervantes?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cervantes has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
